briefdescription.para: The purpose of this function is to illustrate how to use the XOspiPsv device driver using OSPI flash devices greater than or equal to 128Mb.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads data in either DMA or DAC mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Reads the flash ID and identifies the flash in FCT table.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.

briefdescription.para: This function erases the sectors in the serial Flash connected to the OSPIPSV interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
contains the address of the first sector which needs to be erased.
contains the total size to be erased.
is Pointer to the write buffer (which is to be transmitted)

briefdescription.para: This function writes to the serial Flash connected to the OSPIPSV interface.

detaileddescription.para: All the data put into the buffer must be in the same page of the device with page boundaries being on 256 byte boundaries. This can be used in IO or DMA mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
contains the address to write data to in the Flash.
contains the number of bytes to write.
is Pointer to the write buffer (which is to be transmitted)

briefdescription.para: This function writes to the serial Flash connected to the OSPIPSV interface.

detaileddescription.para: All the data put into the buffer must be in the same page of the device with page boundaries being on 256 byte boundaries. This can be used when controller is in Linear mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
contains the address to write data to in the Flash.
contains the number of bytes to write.
is Pointer to the write buffer (which is to be transmitted)

memberdef.argsstring: (XOspiPsv *OspiPsvPtr, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 *WriteBfrPtr, u8 *ReadBfrPtr)
memberdef.name: FlashRead

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
contains the address of the first sector which needs to be erased.
contains the total size to be erased.
is Pointer to the write buffer which contains data to be transmitted
is Pointer to the read buffer to which valid received data should be written

briefdescription.para: This functions translates the address based on the type of interconnection.

detaileddescription.para: In case of stacked, this function asserts the corresponding slave select.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
which is to be accessed (for erase, write or read)

simplesect.para: RealAddr is the translated address - for single it is unchanged; for stacked, the lower flash size is subtracted;
In addition to get the actual address to work on flash this function also selects the CS based on the configuration detected.

briefdescription.para: This functions performs a bulk erase operation when the flash device has a single die.

detaileddescription.para: Works for both Spansion and Micron

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
is the pointer to command+address to be sent

briefdescription.para: This functions performs a die erase operation on all the die in the flash device.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses the die erase command for Micron 512Mbit and 1Gbit

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
is the pointer to command+address to be sent

memberdef.argsstring: (XOspiPsv *OspiPsvPtr, u32 ByteCount, u8 Command, u8 *WriteBfrPtr, u8 WrEn)
memberdef.name: FlashRegisterWrite

briefdescription.para: This API enters the flash device into 4 bytes addressing mode.

detaileddescription.para: As per the Micron spec, before issuing the command to enter into 4 byte addr mode, a write enable command is issued.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
is a either 1 or 0 if 1 then enters 4 byte if 0 exits.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if successful.
XST_FAILURE if it fails.

briefdescription.para: This API enters the flash device into Octal DDR mode or exit from octal DDR mode (switches to Extended SPI mode).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
is either 1 or 0 if 1 then enter octal DDR mode if 0 exits.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if successful.
XST_FAILURE if it fails.

briefdescription.para: This function set the Block Protect bits in the Status register of the device.

detaileddescription.para: User has to pass the BP bits[3:0] value as an argument to this function. This function reads the status register and set the corresponding BP bits as requested by the user and write back the status register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
Block protection bits value (4-bit value).
Indicates whether the protected memory area defined by the block protect bits starts from the top or bottom of the memory.

simplesect.para: This function is tested with the Micron device(MT35XU02G).

briefdescription.para: This function test the Block Protect feature for Micron flash by enabling the write protect for the first sector.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use

simplesect.para: This function is tested with the Micron device(MT35XU02G).

briefdescription.para: This function configures the ECC mode in Flash device.

detaileddescription.para: In some flashes by default 2-bit ECC is enabled, which prevents byte-programming( <16-Bytes). This function enables byte-programming by setting the ECC configuration to 1-bit error detection/correction.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.

simplesect.para: This function is specific to some spansion flashes(S28HS02GT).

briefdescription.para: This function configures the Dummy cycles in Flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.

briefdescription.para: Main function to call the OSPIPSV Flash Polled example.
The purpose of this function is to determine the number of lines used for command, address and data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the OSPIPSV driver component to use.
is to tell whether a read or write

simplesect.para: returns value to program the lines for command, address and data.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the OSPIPSV driver (xospipsv) The example writes to flash in IO mode and reads it back in DMA mode.

detaileddescription.para: It runs in polled mode. The hardware which this example runs on, must have an octal serial Flash (Micron) for it to run.
This example has been tested with the Micron Octal Serial Flash (mt35xu01gbba).

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 nsk 02/19/17 First release sk 01/09/19 Updated flash configuration table with supported commands. sk 02/04/19 Add support for SDR+PHY and DDR+PHY modes. 1.0 akm 03/29/19 Fixed data alignment issues on IAR compiler. 1.1 sk 07/23/19 Based on RX Tuning, updated the dummy cycles. sk 08/08/19 Issue device reset to bring back to default state. 1.3 sk 05/27/20 Added Stacked mode support. 1.4 sk 02/18/21 Added support for Macronix flash and DualByte commands. 1.7 sk 06/28/22 Added Block Protection test for Micron flash. 1.9 sb 06/06/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 1.10 akm 01/31/24 Use OSPI controller reset for resetting flash device. 1.10 sb 02/09/24 Add support for Infineon flash part S28HS02G. 1.11 sb 05/02/24 Add support for Macronix flash part mx66uw2g345gxrix0. 1.12 akm 01/01/25 Pass valid write command in DAC mode. 1.12 sb 03/12/25 Fixed gcc and g++ warnings. 1.14 vlt 12/15/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
